originalTitle: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
title: Africa's Big News: Our Oldest Leader Gets a Helper!
content:
Jambo, my amazing friends across Africa! Welcome to your special news report, brought to you from the heart of our beautiful continent. Today, we have some exciting and very important news from a country called Cameroon. It’s a story about a very important leader and a brand new helper!

Meet President Biya

Imagine someone who has a very, very big job. That’s President Paul Biya. He is the leader of Cameroon, a wonderful country right here in Africa. Just like your school principal is the leader of your school, a President is the leader of a whole country! President Biya is 93 years old. Wow! Can you imagine being 93? That’s much, much older than your parents or even your grandparents! He is known as the world's oldest leader. He has been helping to lead Cameroon for a very, very long time. He won an election last year. This means the people of Cameroon chose him to be their President again. It was his eighth time! That's a lot of times to be chosen to lead.

Cameroon: Our African Home

Let's talk a little about Cameroon! It's a country in West Africa, with beautiful green forests and sunny beaches. Many different animals live there, like clever chimpanzees and colourful birds. People in Cameroon speak different languages, including English and French. Did you know? Cameroon is sometimes called 'Africa in miniature' because it has so many different kinds of landscapes, like mountains, deserts, and rainforests, all in one country!

A Very Long Time Leading

Now, here’s something super interesting. President Biya has been the leader of Cameroon for 43 years! Can you count to 43? That’s like having 43 birthdays while being the President. That’s longer than many of your parents have even been alive! Think about it: 43 years is a super-duper long time to be doing one very big job. Imagine doing the same chore at home every single day for 43 years! It shows how dedicated he is to his country. Did you know? Some leaders only serve for a few years, like maybe 5 or 10 years, before new elections happen and someone new takes over. President Biya has been leading for much, much longer than that!

What is a Deputy?

So, what's the big news today? After all those years, President Biya is going to get a helper! This helper is called a 'deputy'. What does a deputy do? Imagine you and your friend are building a really big sandcastle. It’s too big for just one person to build, right? So, your friend helps you. They might build one tower while you build another, or help you carry water. That's what a deputy does for a President! A deputy helps the President with all the important work. They can share the big jobs, help make decisions, and make sure everything runs smoothly for the country. It’s like having a co-captain on a big team. Having a deputy means the President will have someone to work closely with, to talk to about ideas, and to help carry the load of leading a whole country. It's a very important role!

Why Now, After So Long?

This is big news because President Biya has been leading Cameroon for 43 years without a deputy! Now, for the very first time in all those years, he will have one. Why now? Well, leading a country is a huge job with many, many responsibilities. As we get older, sometimes we need a little more help, even if we are still very good at our jobs. Getting a deputy means President Biya will have extra support to make sure Cameroon keeps growing and doing well. It’s a smart way to make sure that the country's work always gets done, and that there are always two heads working together for the people. Just like when you and your friend work together, things often get done faster and better!

Big News for Cameroon!

So, the really big news is that the world’s oldest leader, President Paul Biya of Cameroon, is getting a deputy for the very first time in his 43 years of leading. This means more teamwork and more hands to help guide the country. It’s an exciting time for Cameroon, as this new helper will bring fresh energy and support to the President’s office. It shows that even the most experienced leaders sometimes welcome a helping hand to serve their people even better. We hope that this new partnership will bring many good things for the children and families of Cameroon. Having good leaders and good helpers makes a country strong!

Did You Know? Fun Facts!

* Did you know that 'Jambo' means 'Hello' in Swahili, a language spoken in many parts of East Africa? * Did you know that leaders around the world work in big buildings called 'palaces' or 'presidential offices'? These are where they make important decisions for their countries. * Did you know that having a helper, or deputy, is common in many countries? It helps to share the big responsibility of leading a nation! * Did you know that Africa is the second-largest continent in the world, full of amazing countries, people, and wildlife? That's all for today's special African news report, my friends! Keep learning and stay curious about our amazing continent and its leaders. Kwaheri (that means Goodbye in Swahili!) for now!
summary: President Paul Biya of Cameroon, the world's oldest leader, will get a deputy to help him for the first time in his 43 years of leading the country.
whyMatters: This is important because leading a country is a very big job, and having a helper (a deputy) means the President will have extra support to make sure important decisions are made and the country keeps running smoothly for everyone.
